description: This license covers all services offered by the facility
The pharmacy is licensed by a relevant licensing body
The laboratory is licensed by a relevant licensing body
The diagnostic imaging service is licensed by a relevant licensing body of radiation control council or equivalent body
The organisation ensures that a qualified individual manages the facility
The facility manager ensures that approved policies are implemented
The facility manager ensures compliance with applicable laws and Regulations
The facility manager implements process to manage and control human, financial and other resources
Contracts and other arrangements are monitored to ensure that the terms of the contracts are met
There is a documented budgeting process
There is an inventory, which is checked according to policy
The leaders are collectively responsible for ensuring that the mission statement is known to all personnel, patients, carers and the community served
The health facilities manager promoted networking with the leaders of other relevant organisations in the community
The management is aware of and accesses services from other provider facilities operating in the area
The health facilities manager ensures that policies and procedures guide and support the activities and management of the health facility
There is a mechanism for ensuring that polices are known and implemented
A suitably qualified individual is designated to control the ordering, storage, distribution and control of equipment and supplies used in the organisation
There is a system for ensuring that equipment and supplies are ordered, available, correctly stored and distributed
There is a system for monitoring the quality of goods delivered
Secure adequate storage facilities are available
Records of goods received and goods issued are available
There is a system for disposing of expired stock, including pharmaceuticals
There is a relevant training programme to equip the personnel with the necessary competencies for designing, implementing and evaluating a quality management and improvement programme
The health centre’s manager and personnel collaborate to plan and carry out the quality improvement and patient safety programme
The health centre’s manager and personnel monitor quality of data collected by the institution
The quality management and improvement programme reflects all components and quality activities in relation to standard/indicator development, monitoring/evaluation and remedial action
There is a process for reviewing patient care
There is a process for reviewing medication use
There is a process for monitoring patient satisfaction with the care process, the care environment and the facility's personnel
There is a relevant/appropriate system for reporting on quality management and improvement matters and communicating with all stakeholders concerned Criteria
There are documented processes for staffing the health facility. 8.1.1. 1 8.1.1. 5 8.1.1. 6 9.1.3. 2
The desired education, qualifications, skills and knowledge are defined for all personnel members
Details of the organisation’s absenteeism, sickness rates and personnel turnover rates are recorded and analysed to allow for informed decision making by the management of organisation
Details of the personal establishment (i.e. available posts, filled and vacant posts) are recorded and analysed to allow for informed decision making by the organisation’s management
Each employee has a written job description and performance agreement, which defines their responsibilities
Each staff member signs their job description and performance agreement to show that they accept it
There is a process for evaluating and verifying the credentials (licence, education, training and experience) of medical practitioners
There is a process for evaluating and verifying the credentials (licence, education, training and experience) of nurses and other health professionals
A determination is made about the annual registration of the individual to provide patient care services
There are written programmes for orientating and inducting personnel to the health facility
The health facility has a coordinated plan for in-service education
All health facility personnel are provided with on-going in-service education, training and development
Personnel competencies, where relevant, are assessed and recorded before and after in- service training and development
Where applicable, relevant charters, laws and regulations are included in organisational policies regarding patient and family rights
The personnel are trained on the policies and procedures and their participation in care processes
Policies and procedures to prevent the loss or misuse of patient information are implemented

The health facility identifies and establishes relationships with community resources which support continuing health promotion and disease prevention education. There is a mechanism to allow complaints to be heard and acted upon
Policies and procedures guide the personnel in the process of obtaining informed consent
The health facility has access to Emergency Medical Services (EMS)
Information on services, hours of operation and processes for obtaining care is provided to agencies and referral sources in the community and to the population served
The health facility uses a health information system that facilitates the collection and utilisation of data
Data is aggregated, analysed and transformed into useful information
The health facility contributes to external reference databases when required by laws or regulations
The performance of the facility on identified priority indicators forms part of the discussions at regular staff meetings
The performance of the facility on identified priority indicators forms part of the discussions at meetings with community representatives
Data is analysed and used to provide relevant information for improving the clinical service
There are written policies and procedures relating to the type of patient record used, e.g. carry cards or other health
Policies specify the records or registers relating to the visits of each patient to be kept by the health facility
The patients' records are up to date to ensure the transfer of the latest information between care providers
A document audit process regularly reviews patient records
The health facility manager ensures that policies and procedures are implemented to guide the personnel
Policies and procedures relate to the safeguarding of information in the record against loss, damage, breach of confidentiality or use by unauthorised persons

There are documented risk management processes for identifying all risks (physical environmental, medico-legal, operational, etc) relating to organisational processes and systems, personnel, patients, visitors and physical facilities
Managers and leaders ensure the development and implementation of written policies and procedures for risk management processes and activities
On-going in -service training of all personnel in these policies, procedures and risk management principles, including reporting of adverse events, is documented
There is a system for monitoring negative incidents/near misses/ adverse (sentinel) events and it includes the documentation of interventions and responses to recorded incidents
Where applicable, legislation regarding occupational health services is implemented
Written policies and procedures on all aspects of health and safety guide the personnel in maintaining a safe work environment
There is effective control of access to restricted areas in the facility, e.g. laboratory, pharmacy, etc
Documented certification is available from the relevant authority to show that the facility complies with applicable laws and regulations in relation to fire safety (e.g. fire clearance certificate)
Fire fighting equipment is regularly inspected and serviced at least annually; the date of the service is recorded on the apparatus
Regular in -service training is given to all personnel in the field of infection control and is documented
Infection Control Program is monitored through a document audit process
The organisation uses risk, rate and trend information to design or modify processes to reduce nosocomial infections to the lowest possible levels
There is a waste management system, consistent with current local bylaws and regulations
Radiology services are available for the level of care provided
Laboratory services are available for the level of care provided
Ultrasound services are available for the level of care provided
Patient and staff accommodation in the outpatient service is adequate for the personnel to provide patient care
The required furniture and equipment is available in accordance with established lists and is functioning properly
Oxygen cylinders are stored in accordance with local safety standards
Waiting times are monitored as part of the organisation's quality management and improvement programme and kept to the minimum
Policies and procedures for assessing patients on arrival and during ongoing care are implemented
Current clinical guidelines relevant to the organisation's patients and services are used to standardise care processes
Case reviews are undertaken to assess the quality of treatment and care of patients requiring emergency care
The health facility has a policy on resuscitation which includes the level at which resuscitation is provided, by whom, and training and equipment requirements
The availability of resuscitation equipment and medicines with clear instructions is specified in the organisation's policy on resuscitation
The personnel are trained in resuscitation and records are kept of their participation in such training
Records of these checks are kept, with reports on problems experienced, advice given, and any remedial action taken
Response time standards are monitored against national laws, regulations, policies or guidelines
The individuals who provide patient care in the ambulance services have the required training and experience
Policies and procedures for holding patients for observation are implemented
Arrangements are in place to ensure that adequate referral services are available
The personnel show evidence of education for and competence in providing a contraceptive service
The personnel who are authorised to insert intrauterine contraceptive devices show evidence of current training and competence in the procedure
The personnel show evidence of education and competence in the provision of antenatal care
A registered/professional nurse with midwifery training/experience is present at every birth
At least one person who is competent in the management of maternal and neonatal emergencies is available for consultation at all times
The personnel show evidence of education for and competence in providing postnatal care
The individuals responsible for the patient's care are designated
Patient and staff accommodation in the service is adequate to meet patient care needs
Resuscitation equipment is available in accordance with the policies of the organisation
Policies and procedures for nursing care are available and are followed as indicated in the statement of intent above
Guidelines are used in clinical monitoring as part of a structured clinical audit
The organisation educates health professionals in assessing and managing pain
A senior professional, who is suitably qualified and/or experienced, is in charge of the theatre and recovery area

Operating theatre rosters ensure that registered nurses with suitable qualifications and/or experience are present during all shifts, for theatre duties, anaesthetic assistance, and for recovery room duties
Anaesthesia is administered only by qualified practitioners, who are privileged by the organisation to do so
Theatre is at least one suitably trained and/or experienced anaesthetic nurse per operating theatre
Nurses, who are trained in recovery room care, are available until the patient has fully recovered
The design of the operating theatre complex provides space for the reception, anaesthesia, surgery, recovery, and observation of patients
The provision and use of anaesthetic mixture components and other peri-operative medication complies with the guidelines of a professional society or similar reputable professional body
Emergency resuscitation equipment and supplies are available
Where ethylene oxide is used as a sterilising agent, the installation complies with relevant safety standards and legislation
There are written policies and procedures to guide the activities of the theatre and the anaesthetic service
During the post-anaesthetic recovery period, patients receive monitoring appropriate to their condition
The laboratory services are organised and provided in a manner that meets applicable national standards, laws and regulations
Laboratory results are validated and include unique patient identity, date of testing/reporting, name and location of the requesting medical practitioner
The laboratory is under the direction of a qualified individual
Qualified individuals are assigned to perform and supervise the provided laboratory services
There are enough staff members to meet service needs
On-going in-service training is provided to all personnel members
Records of the training provided are kept for each personnel member
The available supplies, consumables, reagents, chemicals and kits are sufficient for projected activities
All reagents are stored in a lockable storage room or cupboard
All reagents are checked for expiry dates
There is a documented stock management system that keeps track of current stock
Policies and procedures (SOPs) for handling specimens are implemented
Policies and procedures (SOPs) regarding reporting and reviewing results are implemented
There is a documented quality control system
The selection of an outside source is based on an acceptable record and compliance with applicable laws and regulations
Written policies and procedures address compliance with applicable standards, laws and regulations
A copy of the local rules relating to the current Ionising Radiation regulations is available and the requirements are met
A radiation protection supervisor is identified and available to assist a radiation protection inspector in complying with the Ionising Radiation regulations
The organisation identifies the equipment required for the projected activities
Essential quantities of film, reagents and supplies are available
A qualified individual manages the diagnostic imaging service
Those individuals who may perform X-ray procedures and those who may interpret and report the results are identified

There is a mechanism that ensures that procedures are performed only by radiographers, radiologists or specially trained doctors and other persons who are authorised to do so by a radiation protection advisor
Quality control procedures are implemented
A qualified individual manages the ultrasound service
Those individuals who may perform ultrasound procedures and those who may interpret and report the results are identified
Ultrasound procedures are performed only by individuals with specific training
Quality control procedures are implemented
A designated individual, who is suitably qualified, has clearly defined responsibilities and accountability for all aspects of the pharmaceutical service
Individuals who dispense medications act in accordance with legislation affecting pharmacy practice and current pharmaceutical, medical and nursing guidelines
Secure facilities for storage of medications include, but are not limited to, lockable storage facilities, ceiling cages, burglar guards, and alarm systems with key pads
Policies and procedures are developed and implemented for identified processes, which include at least those from a) to h) in the intent above
Policies and procedures are implemented to ensure that medications are procured according to national guidelines regarding specific agents and approved suppliers

Policies and procedures are implemented to ensure medications are transported to the facility in accordance with manufacturers guidelines, with specific emphasis on maintaining cold chain requirements

Policies and procedures are implemented to ensure that medications are dispensed on the written instructions/ prescription of the designated health care worker who is qualified and/ or experienced in their use
There is a list of the medications stocked in the organisation or readily available from outside sources
Medications controlled by law or organisational policy are accurately accounted for in a specific register
All pharmaceuticals, vaccines or medical consumables are regularly checked for expiry dates and checks are recorded

An inventory management system, manual (stock cards) or automated, is in place and functioning appropriately, e.g. to monitor and control stock losses. under quarantine, e.g. expired stock, compounded products
Separate designated storage areas are provided for materials
Only those permitted by national laws and regulations prescribe medication
Prescriptions conform to legal requirements
A register is maintained of all medicines dispensed
Prescriptions are securely stored in accordance with legislation or organisational policy
A designated, competent individual is responsible for supervising the maintenance of buildings, plant and installations
Written policies and procedures guide the organisation’s personnel in the implementation of all service-related requirements
The organisation plans and budgets for upgrading or replacing systems, buildings or components needed for the continued operation of a safe and effective facility
Regular inspections of all buildings, plant, installations and machinery are documented
There is documented evidence that relevant personnel are regularly trained to use/operate electrical supply systems and to access power in emergencies
Sufficient fuel, e.g. diesel, is available to provide power for twenty four (24) hours
Regular and/or emergency water supplies, including drinkable water, are available twenty four (24) hours a day, seven days a week in all essential areas
There is documented evidence that relevant personnel are regularly trained to ensure that all operations to secure safe water are properly performed
Medical gas (oxygen, nitrous oxide and medical air) supplies are available according to the operational requirements of the facility
Medical gas supply systems comply with safety standards
Piped vacuum systems are regularly tested, and these tests are documented
A designated individual supervises the management of medical equipment in the organisation
Policies and procedures guide the management of medical equipment
The supply of medical equipment is adequate to meet the needs of the service
Records are kept of the checking and maintenance of medical equipment
Policies and procedures guide the management of ICT equipment
A designated individual supervises the management of ICT equipment in the organisation
The supply of ICT equipment is adequate to meet the needs of the services
There is documented evidence that relevant personnel are regularly trained to use/operate ICT equipment
A suitably qualified or experienced person manages/supervises the service
The departmental manager ensures that written policies and procedures are available to guide the personnel in all service-related aspects
The responsibilities of the manager/supervisor are defined in writing

Personnel employed by the organisation are managed in terms of the employer's policies and procedures relating to job descriptions, orientation and induction, in service training and staff performance appraisals
The manager has established an orientation and induction programme for service personnel
The organisation ensures that contracted personnel are oriented to relevant organisational policies and procedures
The Organisation ensures that contracted personnel participates in relevant organisational in-service training programmes (eg; Infection control, Health and Safety)
The food service area meets health and safety regulations
A written agreement is available where the services are outsourced
A suitably qualified and/or experienced person manages/supervises the service
The responsibilities of the manager/supervisor are defined in writing
The organisation ensures that contracted personnel are oriented to relevant organisational policies and procedures

Personnel employed by the organisation are managed in terms of the employer's policies and procedures relating to job descriptions, orientation and induction, in service training and staff performance appraisals
Contracted personnel are managed as determined in the written service agreement
A written agreement is available where the service is outsourced
A suitably qualified and/or experienced person manages/supervises the service
The responsibilities of the manager/supervisor are defined in writing
The departmental manager ensures that written policies and procedures are available to guide the personnel in all service-related aspects

Personnel employed by the organisation are managed in terms of the employer's policies and procedures relating to job descriptions, orientation and induction, in service training and staff performance appraisals
Contracted personnel are managed as determined in the written service agreement
The organisation ensures that contracted personnel participate in relevant organisational in-service training programmes (e.g. infection control, health and safety)
Adequate and secure storage areas are available for equipment and chemicals
Waste is segregated in accordance with policies, procedures and municipal by laws
